title: Crunchy Italian Dried Apricot Crumble
description: A spicy and romantic dessert featuring dried apricots roasted with spices and topped with a dairy-free crumble.

ingredients.name: sous-vide bag
ingredients.notes: suitable for sous-vide at 150°F (65.5°C) for 2 hours

ingredients.name: almond flour
ingredients.notes: or oat flour for gluten-free option

ingredients.name: pitted dates
ingredients.notes: optional, add to sweeten and bind

instructions: Prepare the sous-vide bag by placing the dried apricots in a vacuum-sealed bag with any desired spices like cinnamon and cayenne pepper, then seal it.
Place the bag in a sous-vide water bath set at 150°F (65.5°C) for two hours to slowly cook the fruit evenly without drying them out.
Once cooked, remove the apricots from the bag and let cool slightly.
In a mixing bowl, combine the unsweetened applesauce, almond flour, pitted dates (if using), and vanilla extract. Mix until well combined and smooth.
Once cooled, break the apricots into small pieces and distribute them evenly in a baking dish or shallow pan.
Pour the crumble mixture over the chopped apricots and gently spread it out so that all apricot pieces are coated.
Bake for about 20-30 minutes or until golden brown on top and bubbly around the edges. Keep an eye on them to prevent burning, especially if using pitted dates which can release more moisture.
Let cool slightly before serving. Serve warm with a scoop of dairy-free ice cream or almond milk custard for extra indulgence.
